pi_items.source: task.json description field (line 5) explicitly states 'The channel should be named Patient Admission Channel'. Confirmed in README.md (line 90) task specification.

task.json description field (line 5) explicitly states 'TCP port 6661'. Confirmed in README.md (line 91) stating 'TCP Listener on port 6661'. Also confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 32) listing available HL7 ports: 6661, 6662, 6663.

HL7 sample file hl7-v2.3-adt-a01-1.hl7 contains 'ADT^A01^ADT_A01'. ADT stands for 'Admit, Discharge, Transfer' (verified via web search of HL7 specifications). A01 represents 'Patient Admit' event. README.md (line 79) confirms this file is available and (line 96) mentions 'Send ADT^A01 message through a pre-created channel'. Web search confirms ADT-A01 is patient admission message type.

README.md (line 73) states 'Source: [Work-In-Progress-For-Health/hl7-v2-examples](https://github.com/Work-In-Progress-For-Health/hl7-v2-examples)'. Web search confirmed this is a public GitHub repository containing HL7 v2 example messages.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating and deploying a 'Patient Admission Channel' in NextGen Connect Integration Engine using HL7 ADT^A01 (Admit, Discharge, Transfer - Patient Admission) message format. The channel should listen on TCP port 6661 using MLLP framing and write received messages to files. The data used is synthetic sample HL7 v2.3 messages from the Work-In-Progress-For-Health/hl7-v2-examples GitHub repository, containing test patient data (names like KLEINSAMPLE, BARRY Q JR) and fake facility identifiers (XYZHospC, XYZImgCtr).
